summary: BUG FIX VERIFIED. Editorial scrapbook portfolio for Frenny Sheth renders end-to-end on the preview URL. The previous blank cream screen issue (caused by an orphaned <DoodleStar /> JSX usage after removing its import) is fully resolved. Captured pageerror and console error listeners during load: ZERO uncaught errors, ZERO ReferenceError / 'is not defined' messages. All regressions from the request pass.

verification_results.masthead_the_frenny_journal_present: true (uppercase 'THE FRENNY JOURNAL' visible — case-insensitive match)
verification_results.hero_subtitle_text_check: I ask, why?, useful UX skill all present in [data-testid='hero-subtitle']
verification_results.flip_through_my_work_annotation_present: true (visible with up-left arrow ↖ next to text, see screenshot)
verification_results.contact_root_visible_with_heading: true (heading is 'Write me a letter.' with 'CHAPTER EIGHT · SAY HELLO' subhead; full letter form + side links render)

critical_code_review_comments: Hero.jsx imports are now clean: { DoodleArrow, DoodleHeart, CoffeeRing }. Orphaned <DoodleStar /> was correctly replaced. Recommend adding ESLint 'no-undef' to CI to catch this class of bug at lint time before deploy.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 3 fully verified the DoodleStar bug fix on https://f2f92279-ca3e-4a6e-8241-f7ea75f802a1.preview.emergentagent.com. All Hero / About / Projects / Contact sections render with no JS errors. If retesting, note that the masthead text is rendered in uppercase 'THE FRENNY JOURNAL' so any string comparison should be case-insensitive. The annotation arrow direction='nw' is rendered as a visual SVG inside <Annotation> rather than via a data-direction attribute on the DOM — verify visually or by checking the Annotation component props in source.
rca of the issue: Root cause confirmed by main agent: an orphaned <DoodleStar /> JSX usage at line ~179 of /app/frontend/src/sections/Hero.jsx persisted after DoodleStar was removed from the import list during unused-import cleanup. React threw a ReferenceError on module evaluation, causing the entire React tree to fail to mount and leaving only the cream-coloured body background visible. Fix: replaced <DoodleStar /> with <DoodleArrow direction='down' /> which is already imported. Verified — page renders fully and 0 pageerrors captured.
